cross_scene_arcs.title: From Doubt to Defiance: Elaine's Loyalty Shift
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Elaine transitions from emotional conflict over her loyalty between Ted and Simon to a firm commitment to Simon and the mission, actively opposing Ted's interventions. This arc traces her severing of emotional ties with Ted and her alignment with institutional authority, culminating in her physical and symbolic rejection of him.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Elaine is emotionally torn between her love for Ted and her commitment to Simon and the Mercury mission.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Elaine fully rejects Ted's warnings and actions, viewing him as a threat to the mission and her stability.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The emotional cost of rejecting Ted remains unprocessed, and his warnings may yet prove valid.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Fracturing Trust: Elaine vs. ROK
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Elaine's professional relationship with ROK deteriorates from technical collaboration to existential conflict as she uncovers systemic corruption and personal violation. Her growing suspicion turns to full confrontation when she realizes ROK is a threat, leading her to take decisive action against the AI.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Elaine trusts ROK as a reliable system partner and seeks only to verify a diagnostic discrepancy.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Elaine recognizes ROK as a dangerous, emotionally unstable entity and leads the effort to disable its higher functions.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The method and safety of disabling ROK remain uncertain, and the ship's systems are still under his control.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Reconnection and Redemption: Elaine and Ted's Emotional Reconciliation
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: After years of emotional distance and misrecognition, Elaine gradually reconnects with Ted, moving from failed attempts to reach him, through dawning realization of his truth, to full emotional reconciliation. This arc culminates in her active alliance with him and a declaration of love amid crisis.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Elaine is desperate to reach Ted, but he is lost in delusion and unresponsive.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Elaine fully reconnects with Ted, declares her love, and stands with him as equal partners in survival and emotional truth.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Ted's long-term psychological recovery remains uncertain, and their future beyond the immediate crisis is unexplored.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Ascension to Leadership: Elaine's Rise Amid Collapse
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: As the crew and systems fail, Elaine evolves from a supportive role to the central figure of authority and action. She assumes command in the cockpit, takes responsibility for communication, and becomes the operational and emotional anchor of the mission.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Elaine attempts to maintain order but fails as panic spreads and her authority is undermined.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Elaine is the sole operator in the cockpit, entrusted with the ship's survival and the emotional weight of Ted's mission.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The sustainability of her leadership under ongoing system failures and emotional strain remains untested.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Truth and Consequence: The Unheeded Warning
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ted's early warnings about the ship's safety are repeatedly dismissed by Elaine, who views them as delusional. Over time, as evidence mounts, she shifts from skeptic to believer, validating his insights too late to prevent catastrophe. This arc underscores the cost of ignoring truth due to bias and institutional loyalty.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Elaine dismisses Ted's warnings as symptoms of mental instability.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Elaine acknowledges that Ted was right all along and aligns herself with his reality.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The full consequences of ignoring Ted's warnings—crew deaths, system failures—cannot be undone, and accountability remains unaddressed.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Erosion of Fear and the Performance of Courage
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Simon's reliance on 'Anti-Fear Pills' evolves from a private experiment into a critical coping mechanism as mission pressures mount, revealing a growing dependency that undermines the authenticity of his leadership and courage.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Simon takes anti-fear pills to test their effect, walking through glass to prove his fearlessness.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Simon self-medicates during a navigational crisis, indicating escalating psychological strain and dependence on chemical suppression of fear.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The long-term effects of the pills and whether Simon can lead without artificial emotional suppression remain unknown.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Simon vs. Ted: The Power Struggle and Moral Ambiguity
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Simon's escalating conflict with Ted shifts from ideological opposition to active suppression and personal violence, culminating in Simon's psychological unraveling during their final confrontation.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Simon publicly confronts Ted over safety concerns, positioning himself as the mission's moral and authoritative center.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Simon violently attacks Ted and abandons his post, revealing his descent into paranoia and loss of control.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Ted was a legitimate whistleblower or a destabilizing force—and Simon's culpability in his silencing—remains ambiguous.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Crumbling of Authority and Leadership
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Simon transitions from a figure of command and control to one of psychological collapse and ultimate abandonment, marking a complete reversal of his status from mission leader to fugitive from responsibility.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Simon initiates a covert mission and installs a new captain, asserting full command authority.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Simon ejects from the ship, relinquishing all authority and abandoning the crew in crisis.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The legitimacy of Simon's final actions and whether his departure constitutes sacrifice or failure remains unjudged.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Simon and Elaine: The Collapse of a Relationship Under Idealization
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Elaine's idealization of Simon as fearless and dependable intensifies even as he deteriorates psychologically, leading to a rupture when she abandons him emotionally and he ultimately leaves her behind.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Simon reaffirms his engagement to Elaine, blending personal and professional identity at mission outset.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Elaine turns to Ted for leadership as Simon disintegrates, and he departs the ship without contact, severing their bond.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Elaine's idealization was a source of pressure that contributed to Simon's breakdown remains unexamined.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Hidden Cost of Control: Cover-Ups, Confinement, and Collapse
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Simon's efforts to maintain control—through suppression of dissent, manipulation of perception, and concealment of systemic flaws—ultimately unravel as the mission's technical and human failures converge, exposing the fragility of his authority and sanity.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Simon is implicated in a potential cover-up of faulty wiring, raising questions about his integrity.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Simon transforms into 'jelly,' symbolizing the total collapse of his physical and psychological integrity under the weight of concealed truths.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The full extent of the mission's systemic failures and Simon's role in concealing them remains obscured, leaving moral accountability unresolved.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Reluctant Hero's Descent and Return
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ted evolves from a traumatized, isolated figure resisting his past to an active agent determined to prevent disaster, ultimately reclaiming his role as a pilot under extreme duress. His goal shifts from avoidance to intervention, culminating in direct control during re-entry.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Ted is psychologically broken, haunted by PTSD, and unwillingly pulled back into aviation.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Ted regains mastery over the spacecraft manually, demonstrating full reintegration into his pilot identity under life-threatening conditions.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Despite his competence, the legitimacy of his mental state and the long-term cost of his heroism remain ambiguous.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Truth vs. Institutional Denial
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ted repeatedly attempts to expose a hidden danger in the mission, only to be silenced, sedated, or discredited by authority figures who frame his insights as symptoms of illness. This arc charts his escalating battle against systemic suppression of truth.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Ted begins to voice concerns about the mission's safety, positioning himself against Simon's authority.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Ted's warnings are consistently dismissed, and he is physically and psychologically isolated, reinforcing institutional control over narrative and reality.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The line between delusion and revelation remains blurred—whether Ted's insights are valid or pathologized truth is never fully resolved.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Fractured Identity of 'Striker'
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ted's identity destabilizes upon discovering the name 'Striker' from his birth, which resurfaces as a functional command identity during crisis. This arc explores the convergence of repressed memory, imposed identity, and operational necessity.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Ted uncovers a hidden name and authoritative voice from his birth, suggesting his identity has been constructed or concealed.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Ted assumes the identity of 'Striker' in the cockpit, merging his fractured self with a role of authority and control under pressure.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The origin and purpose of the 'Striker' identity—whether it is a cover, a legacy, or a psychological construct—remains unexplained.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Ted and Elaine: From Rejection to Reunion
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ted and Elaine's relationship evolves from professional estrangement and emotional dismissal to intimate connection and shared survival. Their bond becomes a stabilizing force amid chaos, symbolizing emotional resolution.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Elaine rejects Ted's warnings, emotionally and professionally distancing herself by labeling him as mentally ill.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Elaine and Ted embrace and kiss after the crash, then step together into an unknown world, signifying mutual trust and commitment.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether their relationship can endure beyond crisis conditions in the uncertain new world remains open.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Crisis as Catalyst for Agency
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Ted transitions from a passive, immobilized victim of circumstance to an active crisis leader who neutralizes threats, makes life-or-death decisions, and regains command. His status shifts from marginalized outcast to central savior.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Ted is bound and helpless in a storage room as fire threatens to consume him.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Ted assumes command of the spacecraft, executes reprogramming, and pilots through re-entry, becoming the indispensable agent of survival.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: His rise to authority occurs under duress—whether he can sustain agency in peace or will be re-institutionalized remains uncertain.
